category|Category:Ohari-jinja}} [[JA:尾針神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226111}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ohari Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ohari-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalink|Okayama|Q200078}}{{jalink|Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|Kita Ward}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|29.25|N|133|54|30.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ohari Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{jalink|shinmei-zukuri|Q930545}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 20|Q2959}} and {{jalink|October 21|Q2960|21st}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ohari Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ohari-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ohari Shrine''' (おはりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|Kita Ward}}, {{jalink|Okayama|Q200078}}, {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity * '''{{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}}''' - ancestral deity of the {{jalink|Owari clan|Q11465311}} ; Associated deity * {{jalink|Ogetsuhime|Q3261482|Ogetsuhime-no-Mikoto}} Regarding the deity, ''{{jalink|Kibi Onko Hiroku|Q11412654}}'' also presents theories identifying it as Otsunane-no-Mikoto or {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}}. === History === The shrine stands on a hill to the east-west side of central Okayama. Behind the shrine buildings remains a group of giant rocks regarded as a {{jalink|iwasaka}}, and {{jalink|Haji ware|Q5639938}} and {{jalink|Sue ware|Q7634351}} have been excavated from the eastern slope, indicating long-term habitation. Its founding is unknown, but the area was formerly called "Ifuku-go, Mino District, {{jalink|Kibi Province|Q1155128}}," and is said to have been the settlement of the Ifukube no Muraji (a branch of the {{jalink|Owari clan|Q11465311|Owari no Muraji}}). The shrine is said to have begun by enshrining their ancestral deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines).. Nearby stands another shikinaisha connected to the Owari clan, {{jalink|Oji Harina Mawakahime Shrine|Q17228003}}, suggesting a relationship with that clan. There are theories that the Ifukube migrated west from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-era ''Engishiki Naijinsha / Kokushi Genzai no Jinja'' ("Ohari Shrine" section in ''Nihon no Kamigami'')., and conversely theories that the Owari clan migrated east from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Did the ancient Owari clan migrate east from Kibi?" (''Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walk'', Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. In the mid-{{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' lists "Ohari Shrine, Mino District, {{jalink|Bizen Province|Q1053688}}" as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and this shrine is considered one of its {{jalink|disputed identifications|Q135022904}}. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the exact location of that shikinaisha was said to be unknown, and some documents of that era state that the shrine land of Sakaori-no-miya (now {{jalink|Okayama Shrine|Q11048799}}) was the original precinct. Even now, Ohari Shrine is enshrined among the subordinate shrines of Okayama Shrine, and is regarded as another disputed identification. Until the early Meiji period it was called "Kurioka-no-miya"''Nihon no Kamigami'', section on Ohari Shrine.. In Meiji it was renamed "Ohari Shrine." Under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, it was ranked a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Precincts === File:Ohari-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Ohari-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Ohari-jinja shinmon.JPG|Shrine gate File:Ohari-jinja iwasaka.JPG|Iwasaka remains
Located behind the shrine buildings. === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Ohari-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Subsidiary shrines]] * Inari Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama-no-Kami}} * Wakamiya - deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}} * Kikirimiya - deity: {{jalink|Kukunochi|Q10749920|Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} * Kojin Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Shimenomiya - deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami}} * Tenmangu - deity: {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} * Mae-gozen-miya - deity: {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} === Festivals === * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of May) * Summer festival ({{jalink|July 29|Q2717}}) * Grand annual festival ({{jalink|October 20|Q2959}} and {{jalink|October 21|Q2960|21st}}) === Site information === ; Location * {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalink|Okayama|Q200078}}{{jalink|Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|Kita Ward}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Okayama Station|Q801293}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture'' ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}), section on Ohari Shrine in Okayama * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Nihon no Kamigami - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 2 Sanyo/Shikoku'' ({{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}), section on Ohari Shrine === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ohari-jinja}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:岡山市の神社]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:備前国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:尾張氏|社おはりしんしや]] [[Category:岡山市北区の建築物]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

category|Category:Ohari-jinja}} [[JA:尾針神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226111}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ohari Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ohari-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalink|Okayama|Q200078}}{{jalink|Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|Kita Ward}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|29.25|N|133|54|30.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ohari Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{jalink|shinmei-zukuri|Q930545}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 20|Q2959}} and {{jalink|October 21|Q2960|21st}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ohari Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ohari-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ohari Shrine''' (おはりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|Kita Ward}}, {{jalink|Okayama|Q200078}}, {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity * '''{{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}}''' - ancestral deity of the {{jalink|Owari clan|Q11465311}} ; Associated deity * {{jalink|Ogetsuhime|Q3261482|Ogetsuhime-no-Mikoto}} Regarding the deity, ''{{jalink|Kibi Onko Hiroku|Q11412654}}'' also presents theories identifying it as Otsunane-no-Mikoto or {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}}. === History === The shrine stands on a hill to the east-west side of central Okayama. Behind the shrine buildings remains a group of giant rocks regarded as a {{jalink|iwasaka}}, and {{jalink|Haji ware|Q5639938}} and {{jalink|Sue ware|Q7634351}} have been excavated from the eastern slope, indicating long-term habitation. Its founding is unknown, but the area was formerly called "Ifuku-go, Mino District, {{jalink|Kibi Province|Q1155128}}," and is said to have been the settlement of the Ifukube no Muraji (a branch of the {{jalink|Owari clan|Q11465311|Owari no Muraji}}). The shrine is said to have begun by enshrining their ancestral deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines).. Nearby stands another shikinaisha connected to the Owari clan, {{jalink|Oji Harina Mawakahime Shrine|Q17228003}}, suggesting a relationship with that clan. There are theories that the Ifukube migrated west from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-era ''Engishiki Naijinsha / Kokushi Genzai no Jinja'' ("Ohari Shrine" section in ''Nihon no Kamigami'')., and conversely theories that the Owari clan migrated east from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Did the ancient Owari clan migrate east from Kibi?" (''Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walk'', Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. In the mid-{{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' lists "Ohari Shrine, Mino District, {{jalink|Bizen Province|Q1053688}}" as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and this shrine is considered one of its {{jalink|disputed identifications|Q135022904}}. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the exact location of that shikinaisha was said to be unknown, and some documents of that era state that the shrine land of Sakaori-no-miya (now {{jalink|Okayama Shrine|Q11048799}}) was the original precinct. Even now, Ohari Shrine is enshrined among the subordinate shrines of Okayama Shrine, and is regarded as another disputed identification. Until the early Meiji period it was called "Kurioka-no-miya"''Nihon no Kamigami'', section on Ohari Shrine.. In Meiji it was renamed "Ohari Shrine." Under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, it was ranked a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Precincts === File:Ohari-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Ohari-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Ohari-jinja shinmon.JPG|Shrine gate File:Ohari-jinja iwasaka.JPG|Iwasaka remains
Located behind the shrine buildings. === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Ohari-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Subsidiary shrines]] * Inari Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama-no-Kami}} * Wakamiya - deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}} * Kikirimiya - deity: {{jalink|Kukunochi|Q10749920|Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} * Kojin Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Shimenomiya - deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami}} * Tenmangu - deity: {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} * Mae-gozen-miya - deity: {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} === Festivals === * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of May) * Summer festival ({{jalink|July 29|Q2717}}) * Grand annual festival ({{jalink|October 20|Q2959}} and {{jalink|October 21|Q2960|21st}}) === Site information === ; Location * {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalink|Okayama|Q200078}}{{jalink|Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|Kita Ward}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Okayama Station|Q801293}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture'' ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}), section on Ohari Shrine in Okayama * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Nihon no Kamigami - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 2 Sanyo/Shikoku'' ({{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}), section on Ohari Shrine === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ohari-jinja}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:岡山市の神社]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:備前国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:尾張氏|社おはりしんしや]] [[Category:岡山市北区の建築物]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

category|Category:Ohari-jinja}} [[JA:尾針神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226111}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ohari Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ohari-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalink|Okayama|Q200078}}{{jalink|Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|Kita Ward}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|29.25|N|133|54|30.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ohari Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{jalink|shinmei-zukuri|Q930545}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 20|Q2959}} and {{jalink|October 21|Q2960|21st}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ohari Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ohari-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ohari Shrine''' (おはりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|Kita Ward}}, {{jalink|Okayama|Q200078}}, {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity * '''{{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}}''' - ancestral deity of the {{jalink|Owari clan|Q11465311}} ; Associated deity * {{jalink|Ogetsuhime|Q3261482|Ogetsuhime-no-Mikoto}} Regarding the deity, ''{{jalink|Kibi Onko Hiroku|Q11412654}}'' also presents theories identifying it as Otsunane-no-Mikoto or {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}}. === History === The shrine stands on a hill to the east-west side of central Okayama. Behind the shrine buildings remains a group of giant rocks regarded as a {{jalink|iwasaka}}, and {{jalink|Haji ware|Q5639938}} and {{jalink|Sue ware|Q7634351}} have been excavated from the eastern slope, indicating long-term habitation. Its founding is unknown, but the area was formerly called "Ifuku-go, Mino District, {{jalink|Kibi Province|Q1155128}}," and is said to have been the settlement of the Ifukube no Muraji (a branch of the {{jalink|Owari clan|Q11465311|Owari no Muraji}}). The shrine is said to have begun by enshrining their ancestral deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines).. Nearby stands another shikinaisha connected to the Owari clan, {{jalink|Oji Harina Mawakahime Shrine|Q17228003}}, suggesting a relationship with that clan. There are theories that the Ifukube migrated west from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-era ''Engishiki Naijinsha / Kokushi Genzai no Jinja'' ("Ohari Shrine" section in ''Nihon no Kamigami'')., and conversely theories that the Owari clan migrated east from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Did the ancient Owari clan migrate east from Kibi?" (''Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walk'', Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. In the mid-{{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' lists "Ohari Shrine, Mino District, {{jalink|Bizen Province|Q1053688}}" as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and this shrine is considered one of its {{jalink|disputed identifications|Q135022904}}. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the exact location of that shikinaisha was said to be unknown, and some documents of that era state that the shrine land of Sakaori-no-miya (now {{jalink|Okayama Shrine|Q11048799}}) was the original precinct. Even now, Ohari Shrine is enshrined among the subordinate shrines of Okayama Shrine, and is regarded as another disputed identification. Until the early Meiji period it was called "Kurioka-no-miya"''Nihon no Kamigami'', section on Ohari Shrine.. In Meiji it was renamed "Ohari Shrine." Under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, it was ranked a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Precincts === File:Ohari-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Ohari-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Ohari-jinja shinmon.JPG|Shrine gate File:Ohari-jinja iwasaka.JPG|Iwasaka remains
Located behind the shrine buildings. === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Ohari-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Subsidiary shrines]] * Inari Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama-no-Kami}} * Wakamiya - deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}} * Kikirimiya - deity: {{jalink|Kukunochi|Q10749920|Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} * Kojin Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Shimenomiya - deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami}} * Tenmangu - deity: {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} * Mae-gozen-miya - deity: {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} === Festivals === * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of May) * Summer festival ({{jalink|July 29|Q2717}}) * Grand annual festival ({{jalink|October 20|Q2959}} and {{jalink|October 21|Q2960|21st}}) === Site information === ; Location * {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalink|Okayama|Q200078}}{{jalink|Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|Kita Ward}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Okayama Station|Q801293}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture'' ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}), section on Ohari Shrine in Okayama * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Nihon no Kamigami - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 2 Sanyo/Shikoku'' ({{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}), section on Ohari Shrine === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ohari-jinja}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:岡山市の神社]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:備前国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:尾張氏|社おはりしんしや]] [[Category:岡山市北区の建築物]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

category|Category:Ohari-jinja}} [[JA:尾針神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226111}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ohari Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ohari-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalink|Okayama|Q200078}}{{jalink|Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|Kita Ward}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|29.25|N|133|54|30.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ohari Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{jalink|shinmei-zukuri|Q930545}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 20|Q2959}} and {{jalink|October 21|Q2960|21st}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ohari Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ohari-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ohari Shrine''' (おはりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|Kita Ward}}, {{jalink|Okayama|Q200078}}, {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity * '''{{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}}''' - ancestral deity of the {{jalink|Owari clan|Q11465311}} ; Associated deity * {{jalink|Ogetsuhime|Q3261482|Ogetsuhime-no-Mikoto}} Regarding the deity, ''{{jalink|Kibi Onko Hiroku|Q11412654}}'' also presents theories identifying it as Otsunane-no-Mikoto or {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}}. === History === The shrine stands on a hill to the east-west side of central Okayama. Behind the shrine buildings remains a group of giant rocks regarded as a {{jalink|iwasaka}}, and {{jalink|Haji ware|Q5639938}} and {{jalink|Sue ware|Q7634351}} have been excavated from the eastern slope, indicating long-term habitation. Its founding is unknown, but the area was formerly called "Ifuku-go, Mino District, {{jalink|Kibi Province|Q1155128}}," and is said to have been the settlement of the Ifukube no Muraji (a branch of the {{jalink|Owari clan|Q11465311|Owari no Muraji}}). The shrine is said to have begun by enshrining their ancestral deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines).. Nearby stands another shikinaisha connected to the Owari clan, {{jalink|Oji Harina Mawakahime Shrine|Q17228003}}, suggesting a relationship with that clan. There are theories that the Ifukube migrated west from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-era ''Engishiki Naijinsha / Kokushi Genzai no Jinja'' ("Ohari Shrine" section in ''Nihon no Kamigami'')., and conversely theories that the Owari clan migrated east from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Did the ancient Owari clan migrate east from Kibi?" (''Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walk'', Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. In the mid-{{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' lists "Ohari Shrine, Mino District, {{jalink|Bizen Province|Q1053688}}" as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and this shrine is considered one of its {{jalink|disputed identifications|Q135022904}}. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the exact location of that shikinaisha was said to be unknown, and some documents of that era state that the shrine land of Sakaori-no-miya (now {{jalink|Okayama Shrine|Q11048799}}) was the original precinct. Even now, Ohari Shrine is enshrined among the subordinate shrines of Okayama Shrine, and is regarded as another disputed identification. Until the early Meiji period it was called "Kurioka-no-miya"''Nihon no Kamigami'', section on Ohari Shrine.. In Meiji it was renamed "Ohari Shrine." Under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, it was ranked a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Precincts === File:Ohari-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Ohari-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Ohari-jinja shinmon.JPG|Shrine gate File:Ohari-jinja iwasaka.JPG|Iwasaka remains
Located behind the shrine buildings. === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Ohari-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Subsidiary shrines]] * Inari Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama-no-Kami}} * Wakamiya - deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}} * Kikirimiya - deity: {{jalink|Kukunochi|Q10749920|Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} * Kojin Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Shimenomiya - deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami}} * Tenmangu - deity: {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} * Mae-gozen-miya - deity: {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} === Festivals === * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of May) * Summer festival ({{jalink|July 29|Q2717}}) * Grand annual festival ({{jalink|October 20|Q2959}} and {{jalink|October 21|Q2960|21st}}) === Site information === ; Location * {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalink|Okayama|Q200078}}{{jalink|Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|Kita Ward}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Okayama Station|Q801293}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture'' ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}), section on Ohari Shrine in Okayama * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Nihon no Kamigami - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 2 Sanyo/Shikoku'' ({{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}), section on Ohari Shrine === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ohari-jinja}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:岡山市の神社]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:備前国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:尾張氏|社おはりしんしや]] [[Category:岡山市北区の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

category|Category:Ohari-jinja}} [[JA:尾針神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226111}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ohari Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ohari-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalink|Okayama|Q200078}}{{jalink|Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|Kita Ward}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|29.25|N|133|54|30.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ohari Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{jalink|shinmei-zukuri|Q930545}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 20|Q2959}} and {{jalink|October 21|Q2960|21st}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ohari Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ohari-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ohari Shrine''' (おはりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|Kita Ward}}, {{jalink|Okayama|Q200078}}, {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity * '''{{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}}''' - ancestral deity of the {{jalink|Owari clan|Q11465311}} ; Associated deity * {{jalink|Ogetsuhime|Q3261482|Ogetsuhime-no-Mikoto}} Regarding the deity, ''{{jalink|Kibi Onko Hiroku|Q11412654}}'' also presents theories identifying it as Otsunane-no-Mikoto or {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}}. === History === The shrine stands on a hill to the east-west side of central Okayama. Behind the shrine buildings remains a group of giant rocks regarded as a {{jalink|iwasaka}}, and {{jalink|Haji ware|Q5639938}} and {{jalink|Sue ware|Q7634351}} have been excavated from the eastern slope, indicating long-term habitation. Its founding is unknown, but the area was formerly called "Ifuku-go, Mino District, {{jalink|Kibi Province|Q1155128}}," and is said to have been the settlement of the Ifukube no Muraji (a branch of the {{jalink|Owari clan|Q11465311|Owari no Muraji}}). The shrine is said to have begun by enshrining their ancestral deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines).. Nearby stands another shikinaisha connected to the Owari clan, {{jalink|Oji Harina Mawakahime Shrine|Q17228003}}, suggesting a relationship with that clan. There are theories that the Ifukube migrated west from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-era ''Engishiki Naijinsha / Kokushi Genzai no Jinja'' ("Ohari Shrine" section in ''Nihon no Kamigami'')., and conversely theories that the Owari clan migrated east from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Did the ancient Owari clan migrate east from Kibi?" (''Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walk'', Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. In the mid-{{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' lists "Ohari Shrine, Mino District, {{jalink|Bizen Province|Q1053688}}" as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and this shrine is considered one of its {{jalink|disputed identifications|Q135022904}}. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the exact location of that shikinaisha was said to be unknown, and some documents of that era state that the shrine land of Sakaori-no-miya (now {{jalink|Okayama Shrine|Q11048799}}) was the original precinct. Even now, Ohari Shrine is enshrined among the subordinate shrines of Okayama Shrine, and is regarded as another disputed identification. Until the early Meiji period it was called "Kurioka-no-miya"''Nihon no Kamigami'', section on Ohari Shrine.. In Meiji it was renamed "Ohari Shrine." Under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, it was ranked a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Precincts === File:Ohari-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Ohari-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Ohari-jinja shinmon.JPG|Shrine gate File:Ohari-jinja iwasaka.JPG|Iwasaka remains
Located behind the shrine buildings. === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Ohari-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Subsidiary shrines]] * Inari Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama-no-Kami}} * Wakamiya - deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}} * Kikirimiya - deity: {{jalink|Kukunochi|Q10749920|Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} * Kojin Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Shimenomiya - deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami}} * Tenmangu - deity: {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} * Mae-gozen-miya - deity: {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} === Festivals === * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of May) * Summer festival ({{jalink|July 29|Q2717}}) * Grand annual festival ({{jalink|October 20|Q2959}} and {{jalink|October 21|Q2960|21st}}) === Site information === ; Location * {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalink|Okayama|Q200078}}{{jalink|Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|Kita Ward}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Okayama Station|Q801293}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture'' ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}), section on Ohari Shrine in Okayama * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Nihon no Kamigami - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 2 Sanyo/Shikoku'' ({{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}), section on Ohari Shrine === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ohari-jinja}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:岡山市の神社]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:備前国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:尾張氏|社おはりしんしや]] [[Category:岡山市北区の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]]

category|Category:Ohari-jinja}} [[JA:尾針神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226111}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ohari Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ohari-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalink|Okayama|Q200078}}{{jalink|Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|Kita Ward}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|29.25|N|133|54|30.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ohari Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{jalink|shinmei-zukuri|Q930545}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 20|Q2959}} and {{jalink|October 21|Q2960|21st}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ohari Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ohari-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ohari Shrine''' (おはりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|Kita Ward}}, {{jalink|Okayama|Q200078}}, {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity * '''{{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}}''' - ancestral deity of the {{jalink|Owari clan|Q11465311}} ; Associated deity * {{jalink|Ogetsuhime|Q3261482|Ogetsuhime-no-Mikoto}} Regarding the deity, ''{{jalink|Kibi Onko Hiroku|Q11412654}}'' also presents theories identifying it as Otsunane-no-Mikoto or {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}}. === History === The shrine stands on a hill to the east-west side of central Okayama. Behind the shrine buildings remains a group of giant rocks regarded as a {{jalink|iwasaka}}, and {{jalink|Haji ware|Q5639938}} and {{jalink|Sue ware|Q7634351}} have been excavated from the eastern slope, indicating long-term habitation. Its founding is unknown, but the area was formerly called "Ifuku-go, Mino District, {{jalink|Kibi Province|Q1155128}}," and is said to have been the settlement of the Ifukube no Muraji (a branch of the {{jalink|Owari clan|Q11465311|Owari no Muraji}}). The shrine is said to have begun by enshrining their ancestral deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines).. Nearby stands another shikinaisha connected to the Owari clan, {{jalink|Oji Harina Mawakahime Shrine|Q17228003}}, suggesting a relationship with that clan. There are theories that the Ifukube migrated west from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-era ''Engishiki Naijinsha / Kokushi Genzai no Jinja'' ("Ohari Shrine" section in ''Nihon no Kamigami'')., and conversely theories that the Owari clan migrated east from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Did the ancient Owari clan migrate east from Kibi?" (''Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walk'', Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. In the mid-{{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' lists "Ohari Shrine, Mino District, {{jalink|Bizen Province|Q1053688}}" as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and this shrine is considered one of its {{jalink|disputed identifications|Q135022904}}. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the exact location of that shikinaisha was said to be unknown, and some documents of that era state that the shrine land of Sakaori-no-miya (now {{jalink|Okayama Shrine|Q11048799}}) was the original precinct. Even now, Ohari Shrine is enshrined among the subordinate shrines of Okayama Shrine, and is regarded as another disputed identification. Until the early Meiji period it was called "Kurioka-no-miya"''Nihon no Kamigami'', section on Ohari Shrine.. In Meiji it was renamed "Ohari Shrine." Under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, it was ranked a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Precincts === File:Ohari-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Ohari-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Ohari-jinja shinmon.JPG|Shrine gate File:Ohari-jinja iwasaka.JPG|Iwasaka remains
Located behind the shrine buildings. === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Ohari-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Subsidiary shrines]] * Inari Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama-no-Kami}} * Wakamiya - deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}} * Kikirimiya - deity: {{jalink|Kukunochi|Q10749920|Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} * Kojin Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Shimenomiya - deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami}} * Tenmangu - deity: {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} * Mae-gozen-miya - deity: {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} === Festivals === * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of May) * Summer festival ({{jalink|July 29|Q2717}}) * Grand annual festival ({{jalink|October 20|Q2959}} and {{jalink|October 21|Q2960|21st}}) === Site information === ; Location * {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalink|Okayama|Q200078}}{{jalink|Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|Kita Ward}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Okayama Station|Q801293}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture'' ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}), section on Ohari Shrine in Okayama * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Nihon no Kamigami - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 2 Sanyo/Shikoku'' ({{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}), section on Ohari Shrine === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ohari-jinja}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:岡山市の神社]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:備前国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:尾張氏|社おはりしんしや]] [[Category:岡山市北区の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Ohari-jinja}} [[JA:尾針神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226111}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ohari Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ohari-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalink|Okayama|Q200078}}{{jalink|Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|Kita Ward}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|29.25|N|133|54|30.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ohari Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{jalink|shinmei-zukuri|Q930545}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 20|Q2959}} and {{jalink|October 21|Q2960|21st}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ohari Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ohari-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ohari Shrine''' (おはりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|Kita Ward}}, {{jalink|Okayama|Q200078}}, {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} {{jalink|disputed identification shrine|Q135022904}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity * '''{{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}}''' - ancestral deity of the {{jalink|Owari clan|Q11465311}} ; Associated deity * {{jalink|Ogetsuhime|Q3261482|Ogetsuhime-no-Mikoto}} Regarding the deity, ''{{jalink|Kibi Onko Hiroku|Q11412654}}'' also presents theories identifying it as Otsunane-no-Mikoto or {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}}. === History === The shrine stands on a hill to the east-west side of central Okayama. Behind the shrine buildings remains a group of giant rocks regarded as a {{jalink|iwasaka}}, and {{jalink|Haji ware|Q5639938}} and {{jalink|Sue ware|Q7634351}} have been excavated from the eastern slope, indicating long-term habitation. Its founding is unknown, but the area was formerly called "Ifuku-go, Mino District, {{jalink|Kibi Province|Q1155128}}," and is said to have been the settlement of the Ifukube no Muraji (a branch of the {{jalink|Owari clan|Q11465311|Owari no Muraji}}). The shrine is said to have begun by enshrining their ancestral deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines).. Nearby stands another shikinaisha connected to the Owari clan, {{jalink|Oji Harina Mawakahime Shrine|Q17228003}}, suggesting a relationship with that clan. There are theories that the Ifukube migrated west from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-era ''Engishiki Naijinsha / Kokushi Genzai no Jinja'' ("Ohari Shrine" section in ''Nihon no Kamigami'')., and conversely theories that the Owari clan migrated east from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Did the ancient Owari clan migrate east from Kibi?" (''Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walk'', Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. In the mid-{{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' lists "Ohari Shrine, Mino District, {{jalink|Bizen Province|Q1053688}}" as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and this shrine is considered one of its {{jalink|disputed identifications|Q135022904}}. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the exact location of that shikinaisha was said to be unknown, and some documents of that era state that the shrine land of Sakaori-no-miya (now {{jalink|Okayama Shrine|Q11048799}}) was the original precinct. Even now, Ohari Shrine is enshrined among the subordinate shrines of Okayama Shrine, and is regarded as another disputed identification. Until the early Meiji period it was called "Kurioka-no-miya"''Nihon no Kamigami'', section on Ohari Shrine.. In Meiji it was renamed "Ohari Shrine." Under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, it was ranked a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Precincts === File:Ohari-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Ohari-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Ohari-jinja shinmon.JPG|Shrine gate File:Ohari-jinja iwasaka.JPG|Iwasaka remains
Located behind the shrine buildings. === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Ohari-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Subsidiary shrines]] * Inari Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama-no-Kami}} * Wakamiya - deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}} * Kikirimiya - deity: {{jalink|Kukunochi|Q10749920|Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} * Kojin Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Shimenomiya - deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami}} * Tenmangu - deity: {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} * Mae-gozen-miya - deity: {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} === Festivals === * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of May) * Summer festival ({{jalink|July 29|Q2717}}) * Grand annual festival ({{jalink|October 20|Q2959}} and {{jalink|October 21|Q2960|21st}}) === Site information === ; Location * {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalink|Okayama|Q200078}}{{jalink|Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|Kita Ward}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Okayama Station|Q801293}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture'' ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}), section on Ohari Shrine in Okayama * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Nihon no Kamigami - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 2 Sanyo/Shikoku'' ({{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}), section on Ohari Shrine === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ohari-jinja}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:岡山市の神社]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:備前国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:尾張氏|社おはりしんしや]] [[Category:岡山市北区の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Ohari-jinja}} [[JA:尾針神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226111}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ohari Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ohari-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|29.25|N|133|54|30.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ohari Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|lt=21st}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ohari Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ohari-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ohari Shrine''' (おはりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity * '''{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411}}''' - ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311}} ; Associated deity * {{ill|lt=Ogetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ogetsuhime-no-Mikoto}} Regarding the deity, ''{{ill|lt=Kibi Onko Hiroku|Q11412654|WD=Q11412654}}'' also presents theories identifying it as Otsunane-no-Mikoto or {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}}. === History === The shrine stands on a hill to the east-west side of central Okayama. Behind the shrine buildings remains a group of giant rocks regarded as a {{ill||lt=iwasaka|UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=Haji ware|Q5639938|WD=Q5639938}} and {{ill|lt=Sue ware|Q7634351|WD=Q7634351}} have been excavated from the eastern slope, indicating long-term habitation. Its founding is unknown, but the area was formerly called "Ifuku-go, Mino District, {{ill|lt=Kibi Province|Q1155128|WD=Q1155128}}," and is said to have been the settlement of the Ifukube no Muraji (a branch of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|lt=Owari no Muraji}}). The shrine is said to have begun by enshrining their ancestral deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines).. Nearby stands another shikinaisha connected to the Owari clan, {{ill|lt=Oji Harina Mawakahime Shrine|Q17228003|WD=Q17228003}}, suggesting a relationship with that clan. There are theories that the Ifukube migrated west from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-era ''Engishiki Naijinsha / Kokushi Genzai no Jinja'' ("Ohari Shrine" section in ''Nihon no Kamigami'')., and conversely theories that the Owari clan migrated east from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Did the ancient Owari clan migrate east from Kibi?" (''Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walk'', Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' lists "Ohari Shrine, Mino District, {{ill|lt=Bizen Province|Q1053688|WD=Q1053688}}" as a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and this shrine is considered one of its {{ill|lt=disputed identifications|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, the exact location of that shikinaisha was said to be unknown, and some documents of that era state that the shrine land of Sakaori-no-miya (now {{ill|lt=Okayama Shrine|Q11048799|WD=Q11048799}}) was the original precinct. Even now, Ohari Shrine is enshrined among the subordinate shrines of Okayama Shrine, and is regarded as another disputed identification. Until the early Meiji period it was called "Kurioka-no-miya"''Nihon no Kamigami'', section on Ohari Shrine.. In Meiji it was renamed "Ohari Shrine." Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, it was ranked a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Precincts === File:Ohari-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Ohari-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Ohari-jinja shinmon.JPG|Shrine gate File:Ohari-jinja iwasaka.JPG|Iwasaka remains
Located behind the shrine buildings. === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Ohari-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Subsidiary shrines]] * Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami}} * Wakamiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849}} * Kikirimiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} * Kojin Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Shimenomiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami}} * Tenmangu - deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} * Mae-gozen-miya - deity: {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} === Festivals === * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of May) * Summer festival ({{ill|lt=July 29|Q2717|WD=Q2717}}) * Grand annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|lt=21st}}) === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Okayama Station|Q801293|WD=Q801293}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), section on Ohari Shrine in Okayama * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Nihon no Kamigami - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 2 Sanyo/Shikoku'' ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}), section on Ohari Shrine === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ohari-jinja}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:岡山市の神社]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:備前国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:尾張氏|社おはりしんしや]] [[Category:岡山市北区の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Ohari-jinja}} [[JA:尾針神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226111}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ohari Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ohari-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|29.25|N|133|54|30.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ohari Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|lt=21st}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ohari Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ohari-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ohari Shrine''' (おはりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity * '''{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411}}''' - ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311}} ; Associated deity * {{ill|lt=Ogetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ogetsuhime-no-Mikoto}} Regarding the deity, ''{{ill|lt=Kibi Onko Hiroku|Q11412654|WD=Q11412654}}'' also presents theories identifying it as Otsunane-no-Mikoto or {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}}. === History === The shrine stands on a hill to the east-west side of central Okayama. Behind the shrine buildings remains a group of giant rocks regarded as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=iwasaka|UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=Haji ware|Q5639938|WD=Q5639938}} and {{ill|lt=Sue ware|Q7634351|WD=Q7634351}} have been excavated from the eastern slope, indicating long-term habitation. Its founding is unknown, but the area was formerly called "Ifuku-go, Mino District, {{ill|lt=Kibi Province|Q1155128|WD=Q1155128}}," and is said to have been the settlement of the Ifukube no Muraji (a branch of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|lt=Owari no Muraji}}). The shrine is said to have begun by enshrining their ancestral deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines).. Nearby stands another shikinaisha connected to the Owari clan, {{ill|lt=Oji Harina Mawakahime Shrine|Q17228003|WD=Q17228003}}, suggesting a relationship with that clan. There are theories that the Ifukube migrated west from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-era ''Engishiki Naijinsha / Kokushi Genzai no Jinja'' ("Ohari Shrine" section in ''Nihon no Kamigami'')., and conversely theories that the Owari clan migrated east from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Did the ancient Owari clan migrate east from Kibi?" (''Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walk'', Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' lists "Ohari Shrine, Mino District, {{ill|lt=Bizen Province|Q1053688|WD=Q1053688}}" as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and this shrine is considered one of its {{ill|lt=disputed identifications|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, the exact location of that shikinaisha was said to be unknown, and some documents of that era state that the shrine land of Sakaori-no-miya (now {{ill|lt=Okayama Shrine|Q11048799|WD=Q11048799}}) was the original precinct. Even now, Ohari Shrine is enshrined among the subordinate shrines of Okayama Shrine, and is regarded as another disputed identification. Until the early Meiji period it was called "Kurioka-no-miya"''Nihon no Kamigami'', section on Ohari Shrine.. In Meiji it was renamed "Ohari Shrine." Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, it was ranked a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Precincts === File:Ohari-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Ohari-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Ohari-jinja shinmon.JPG|Shrine gate File:Ohari-jinja iwasaka.JPG|Iwasaka remains
Located behind the shrine buildings. === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Ohari-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Subsidiary shrines]] * Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami}} * Wakamiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849}} * Kikirimiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} * Kojin Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Shimenomiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami}} * Tenmangu - deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} * Mae-gozen-miya - deity: {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} === Festivals === * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of May) * Summer festival ({{ill|lt=July 29|Q2717|WD=Q2717}}) * Grand annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|lt=21st}}) === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Okayama Station|Q801293|WD=Q801293}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), section on Ohari Shrine in Okayama * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Nihon no Kamigami - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 2 Sanyo/Shikoku'' ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}), section on Ohari Shrine === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ohari-jinja}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:岡山市の神社]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:備前国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:尾張氏|社おはりしんしや]] [[Category:岡山市北区の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Ohari-jinja}} [[JA:尾針神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226111}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ohari Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ohari-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|29.25|N|133|54|30.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ohari Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|lt=21st}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ohari Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ohari-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ohari Shrine''' (おはりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity * '''{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411}}''' - ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311}} ; Associated deity * {{ill|lt=Ogetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ogetsuhime-no-Mikoto}} Regarding the deity, ''{{ill|lt=Kibi Onko Hiroku|Q11412654|WD=Q11412654}}'' also presents theories identifying it as Otsunane-no-Mikoto or {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}}. === History === The shrine stands on a hill to the east-west side of central Okayama. Behind the shrine buildings remains a group of giant rocks regarded as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=iwasaka|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=Haji ware|Q5639938|WD=Q5639938}} and {{ill|lt=Sue ware|Q7634351|WD=Q7634351}} have been excavated from the eastern slope, indicating long-term habitation. Its founding is unknown, but the area was formerly called "Ifuku-go, Mino District, {{ill|lt=Kibi Province|Q1155128|WD=Q1155128}}," and is said to have been the settlement of the Ifukube no Muraji (a branch of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|lt=Owari no Muraji}}). The shrine is said to have begun by enshrining their ancestral deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines).. Nearby stands another shikinaisha connected to the Owari clan, {{ill|lt=Oji Harina Mawakahime Shrine|Q17228003|WD=Q17228003}}, suggesting a relationship with that clan. There are theories that the Ifukube migrated west from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-era ''Engishiki Naijinsha / Kokushi Genzai no Jinja'' ("Ohari Shrine" section in ''Nihon no Kamigami'')., and conversely theories that the Owari clan migrated east from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Did the ancient Owari clan migrate east from Kibi?" (''Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walk'', Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' lists "Ohari Shrine, Mino District, {{ill|lt=Bizen Province|Q1053688|WD=Q1053688}}" as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and this shrine is considered one of its {{ill|lt=disputed identifications|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, the exact location of that shikinaisha was said to be unknown, and some documents of that era state that the shrine land of Sakaori-no-miya (now {{ill|lt=Okayama Shrine|Q11048799|WD=Q11048799}}) was the original precinct. Even now, Ohari Shrine is enshrined among the subordinate shrines of Okayama Shrine, and is regarded as another disputed identification. Until the early Meiji period it was called "Kurioka-no-miya"''Nihon no Kamigami'', section on Ohari Shrine.. In Meiji it was renamed "Ohari Shrine." Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, it was ranked a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Precincts === File:Ohari-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Ohari-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Ohari-jinja shinmon.JPG|Shrine gate File:Ohari-jinja iwasaka.JPG|Iwasaka remains
Located behind the shrine buildings. === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Ohari-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Subsidiary shrines]] * Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami}} * Wakamiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849}} * Kikirimiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} * Kojin Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Shimenomiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami}} * Tenmangu - deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} * Mae-gozen-miya - deity: {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} === Festivals === * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of May) * Summer festival ({{ill|lt=July 29|Q2717|WD=Q2717}}) * Grand annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|lt=21st}}) === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Okayama Station|Q801293|WD=Q801293}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), section on Ohari Shrine in Okayama * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Nihon no Kamigami - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 2 Sanyo/Shikoku'' ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}), section on Ohari Shrine === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ohari-jinja}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:岡山市の神社]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:備前国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:尾張氏|社おはりしんしや]] [[Category:岡山市北区の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Ohari-jinja}} [[JA:尾針神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226111}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ohari Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ohari-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|29.25|N|133|54|30.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ohari Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|lt=21st}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ohari Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ohari-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ohari Shrine''' (おはりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity * '''{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411}}''' - ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311}} ; Associated deity * {{ill|lt=Ogetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ogetsuhime-no-Mikoto}} Regarding the deity, ''{{ill|lt=Kibi Onko Hiroku|Q11412654|WD=Q11412654}}'' also presents theories identifying it as Otsunane-no-Mikoto or {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}}. === History === The shrine stands on a hill to the east-west side of central Okayama. Behind the shrine buildings remains a group of giant rocks regarded as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=iwasaka|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=Haji ware|Q5639938|WD=Q5639938}} and {{ill|lt=Sue ware|Q7634351|WD=Q7634351}} have been excavated from the eastern slope, indicating long-term habitation. Its founding is unknown, but the area was formerly called "Ifuku-go, Mino District, {{ill|lt=Kibi Province|Q1155128|WD=Q1155128}}," and is said to have been the settlement of the Ifukube no Muraji (a branch of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|lt=Owari no Muraji}}). The shrine is said to have begun by enshrining their ancestral deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines).. Nearby stands another shikinaisha connected to the Owari clan, {{ill|lt=Oji Harina Mawakahime Shrine|Q17228003|WD=Q17228003}}, suggesting a relationship with that clan. There are theories that the Ifukube migrated west from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-era ''Engishiki Naijinsha / Kokushi Genzai no Jinja'' ("Ohari Shrine" section in ''Nihon no Kamigami'')., and conversely theories that the Owari clan migrated east from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Did the ancient Owari clan migrate east from Kibi?" (''Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walk'', Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' lists "Ohari Shrine, Mino District, {{ill|lt=Bizen Province|Q1053688|WD=Q1053688}}" as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and this shrine is considered one of its {{ill|lt=disputed identifications|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, the exact location of that shikinaisha was said to be unknown, and some documents of that era state that the shrine land of Sakaori-no-miya (now {{ill|lt=Okayama Shrine|Q11048799|WD=Q11048799}}) was the original precinct. Even now, Ohari Shrine is enshrined among the subordinate shrines of Okayama Shrine, and is regarded as another disputed identification. Until the early Meiji period it was called "Kurioka-no-miya"''Nihon no Kamigami'', section on Ohari Shrine.. In Meiji it was renamed "Ohari Shrine." Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, it was ranked a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Precincts === File:Ohari-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Ohari-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Ohari-jinja shinmon.JPG|Shrine gate File:Ohari-jinja iwasaka.JPG|Iwasaka remains
Located behind the shrine buildings. === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Ohari-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Subsidiary shrines]] * Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami}} * Wakamiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849}} * Kikirimiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto}} * Kojin Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Shimenomiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami}} * Tenmangu - deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} * Mae-gozen-miya - deity: {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} === Festivals === * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of May) * Summer festival ({{ill|lt=July 29|Q2717|WD=Q2717}}) * Grand annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|lt=21st}}) === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Okayama Station|Q801293|WD=Q801293}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), section on Ohari Shrine in Okayama * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Nihon no Kamigami - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 2 Sanyo/Shikoku'' ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}), section on Ohari Shrine === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ohari-jinja}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:岡山市の神社]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:備前国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:尾張氏|社おはりしんしや]] [[Category:岡山市北区の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]
