== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1260005000332 == Commons category (P373) == * Ohari-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岡山県岡山市北区京山2-2-2', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Ohari-jinja}} [[JA:尾針神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226111}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ohari Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ohari-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|29.25|N|133|54|30.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ohari Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|lt=21st|1=October 21}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ohari Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ohari-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ohari Shrine''' (おはりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity * '''{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}''' - ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}} ; Associated deity * {{ill|lt=Ogetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ogetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}} Regarding the deity, ''{{ill|lt=Kibi Onko Hiroku|Q11412654|WD=Q11412654|1=Kibi Onko Hiroku}}'' also presents theories identifying it as Otsunane-no-Mikoto or {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}}. === History === The shrine stands on a hill to the east-west side of central Okayama. Behind the shrine buildings remains a group of giant rocks regarded as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=iwasaka|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=Haji ware|Q5639938|WD=Q5639938|1=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|lt=Sue ware|Q7634351|WD=Q7634351|1=Sue pottery}} have been excavated from the eastern slope, indicating long-term habitation. Its founding is unknown, but the area was formerly called "Ifuku-go, Mino District, {{ill|lt=Kibi Province|Q1155128|WD=Q1155128|1=Kingdom of Kibi}}," and is said to have been the settlement of the Ifukube no Muraji (a branch of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|lt=Owari no Muraji|1=Owari clan}}). The shrine is said to have begun by enshrining their ancestral deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines).. Nearby stands another shikinaisha connected to the Owari clan, {{ill|lt=Oji Harina Mawakahime Shrine|Q17228003|WD=Q17228003|1=Ojiharina Mawakahime Shrine}}, suggesting a relationship with that clan. There are theories that the Ifukube migrated west from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-era ''Engishiki Naijinsha / Kokushi Genzai no Jinja'' ("Ohari Shrine" section in ''Nihon no Kamigami'')., and conversely theories that the Owari clan migrated east from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Did the ancient Owari clan migrate east from Kibi?" (''Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walk'', Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists "Ohari Shrine, Mino District, {{ill|lt=Bizen Province|Q1053688|WD=Q1053688|1=Bizen Province}}" as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and this shrine is considered one of its {{ill|lt=disputed identifications|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the exact location of that shikinaisha was said to be unknown, and some documents of that era state that the shrine land of Sakaori-no-miya (now {{ill|lt=Okayama Shrine|Q11048799|WD=Q11048799|1=Okayama Shrine}}) was the original precinct. Even now, Ohari Shrine is enshrined among the subordinate shrines of Okayama Shrine, and is regarded as another disputed identification. Until the early Meiji period it was called "Kurioka-no-miya"''Nihon no Kamigami'', section on Ohari Shrine.. In Meiji it was renamed "Ohari Shrine." Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precincts === File:Ohari-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Ohari-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Ohari-jinja shinmon.JPG|Shrine gate File:Ohari-jinja iwasaka.JPG|Iwasaka remains
Located behind the shrine buildings. === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Ohari-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Subsidiary shrines]] * Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} * Wakamiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} * Kikirimiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto|1=Kukunochi}} * Kojin Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Shimenomiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami|1=Ame-no-Uzume}} * Tenmangu - deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Mae-gozen-miya - deity: {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} === Festivals === * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of May) * Summer festival ({{ill|lt=July 29|Q2717|WD=Q2717|1=July 29}}) * Grand annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|lt=21st|1=October 21}}) === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Okayama Station|Q801293|WD=Q801293|1=Okayama Station}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), section on Ohari Shrine in Okayama * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Nihon no Kamigami - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 2 Sanyo/Shikoku'' ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}), section on Ohari Shrine === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ohari-jinja}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:岡山市北区の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1260005000332 == Commons category (P373) == * Ohari-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岡山県岡山市北区京山2-2-2', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Ohari-jinja}} [[JA:尾針神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226111}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ohari Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ohari-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|29.25|N|133|54|30.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ohari Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|lt=21st|1=October 21}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ohari Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ohari-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ohari Shrine''' (おはりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity * '''{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}''' - ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}} ; Associated deity * {{ill|lt=Ogetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ogetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}} Regarding the deity, ''{{ill|lt=Kibi Onko Hiroku|Q11412654|WD=Q11412654|1=Kibi Onko Hiroku}}'' also presents theories identifying it as Otsunane-no-Mikoto or {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}}. === History === The shrine stands on a hill to the east-west side of central Okayama. Behind the shrine buildings remains a group of giant rocks regarded as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=iwasaka|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=Haji ware|Q5639938|WD=Q5639938|1=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|lt=Sue ware|Q7634351|WD=Q7634351|1=Sue pottery}} have been excavated from the eastern slope, indicating long-term habitation. Its founding is unknown, but the area was formerly called "Ifuku-go, Mino District, {{ill|lt=Kibi Province|Q1155128|WD=Q1155128|1=Kingdom of Kibi}}," and is said to have been the settlement of the Ifukube no Muraji (a branch of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|lt=Owari no Muraji|1=Owari clan}}). The shrine is said to have begun by enshrining their ancestral deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines).. Nearby stands another shikinaisha connected to the Owari clan, {{ill|lt=Oji Harina Mawakahime Shrine|Q17228003|WD=Q17228003|1=Ojiharina Mawakahime Shrine}}, suggesting a relationship with that clan. There are theories that the Ifukube migrated west from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-era ''Engishiki Naijinsha / Kokushi Genzai no Jinja'' ("Ohari Shrine" section in ''Nihon no Kamigami'')., and conversely theories that the Owari clan migrated east from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Did the ancient Owari clan migrate east from Kibi?" (''Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walk'', Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists "Ohari Shrine, Mino District, {{ill|lt=Bizen Province|Q1053688|WD=Q1053688|1=Bizen Province}}" as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and this shrine is considered one of its {{ill|lt=disputed identifications|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the exact location of that shikinaisha was said to be unknown, and some documents of that era state that the shrine land of Sakaori-no-miya (now {{ill|lt=Okayama Shrine|Q11048799|WD=Q11048799|1=Okayama Shrine}}) was the original precinct. Even now, Ohari Shrine is enshrined among the subordinate shrines of Okayama Shrine, and is regarded as another disputed identification. Until the early Meiji period it was called "Kurioka-no-miya"''Nihon no Kamigami'', section on Ohari Shrine.. In Meiji it was renamed "Ohari Shrine." Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precincts === File:Ohari-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Ohari-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Ohari-jinja shinmon.JPG|Shrine gate File:Ohari-jinja iwasaka.JPG|Iwasaka remains
Located behind the shrine buildings. === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Ohari-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Subsidiary shrines]] * Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} * Wakamiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} * Kikirimiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto|1=Kukunochi}} * Kojin Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Shimenomiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami|1=Ame-no-Uzume}} * Tenmangu - deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Mae-gozen-miya - deity: {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} === Festivals === * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of May) * Summer festival ({{ill|lt=July 29|Q2717|WD=Q2717|1=July 29}}) * Grand annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|lt=21st|1=October 21}}) === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Okayama Station|Q801293|WD=Q801293|1=Okayama Station}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), section on Ohari Shrine in Okayama * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Nihon no Kamigami - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 2 Sanyo/Shikoku'' ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}), section on Ohari Shrine === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ohari-jinja}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1260005000332 == Commons category (P373) == * Ohari-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岡山県岡山市北区京山2-2-2', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Ohari-jinja}} [[JA:尾針神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226111}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ohari Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ohari-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|29.25|N|133|54|30.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ohari Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|lt=21st|1=October 21}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ohari Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ohari-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ohari Shrine''' (おはりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity * '''{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}''' - ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}} ; Associated deity * {{ill|lt=Ogetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ogetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}} Regarding the deity, ''{{ill|lt=Kibi Onko Hiroku|Q11412654|WD=Q11412654|1=Kibi Onko Hiroku}}'' also presents theories identifying it as Otsunane-no-Mikoto or {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}}. === History === The shrine stands on a hill to the east-west side of central Okayama. Behind the shrine buildings remains a group of giant rocks regarded as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=iwasaka|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=Haji ware|Q5639938|WD=Q5639938|1=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|lt=Sue ware|Q7634351|WD=Q7634351|1=Sue pottery}} have been excavated from the eastern slope, indicating long-term habitation. Its founding is unknown, but the area was formerly called "Ifuku-go, Mino District, {{ill|lt=Kibi Province|Q1155128|WD=Q1155128|1=Kingdom of Kibi}}," and is said to have been the settlement of the Ifukube no Muraji (a branch of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|lt=Owari no Muraji|1=Owari clan}}). The shrine is said to have begun by enshrining their ancestral deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines).. Nearby stands another shikinaisha connected to the Owari clan, {{ill|lt=Oji Harina Mawakahime Shrine|Q17228003|WD=Q17228003|1=Ojiharina Mawakahime Shrine}}, suggesting a relationship with that clan. There are theories that the Ifukube migrated west from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-era ''Engishiki Naijinsha / Kokushi Genzai no Jinja'' ("Ohari Shrine" section in ''Nihon no Kamigami'')., and conversely theories that the Owari clan migrated east from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Did the ancient Owari clan migrate east from Kibi?" (''Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walk'', Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists "Ohari Shrine, Mino District, {{ill|lt=Bizen Province|Q1053688|WD=Q1053688|1=Bizen Province}}" as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and this shrine is considered one of its {{ill|lt=disputed identifications|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the exact location of that shikinaisha was said to be unknown, and some documents of that era state that the shrine land of Sakaori-no-miya (now {{ill|lt=Okayama Shrine|Q11048799|WD=Q11048799|1=Okayama Shrine}}) was the original precinct. Even now, Ohari Shrine is enshrined among the subordinate shrines of Okayama Shrine, and is regarded as another disputed identification. Until the early Meiji period it was called "Kurioka-no-miya"''Nihon no Kamigami'', section on Ohari Shrine.. In Meiji it was renamed "Ohari Shrine." Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precincts === File:Ohari-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Ohari-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Ohari-jinja shinmon.JPG|Shrine gate File:Ohari-jinja iwasaka.JPG|Iwasaka remains
Located behind the shrine buildings. === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Ohari-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Subsidiary shrines]] * Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} * Wakamiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} * Kikirimiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto|1=Kukunochi}} * Kojin Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Shimenomiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami|1=Ame-no-Uzume}} * Tenmangu - deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Mae-gozen-miya - deity: {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} === Festivals === * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of May) * Summer festival ({{ill|lt=July 29|Q2717|WD=Q2717|1=July 29}}) * Grand annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|lt=21st|1=October 21}}) === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Okayama Station|Q801293|WD=Q801293|1=Okayama Station}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), section on Ohari Shrine in Okayama * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Nihon no Kamigami - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 2 Sanyo/Shikoku'' ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}), section on Ohari Shrine === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ohari-jinja}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1260005000332 == Commons category (P373) == * Ohari-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岡山県岡山市北区京山2-2-2', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Ohari-jinja}} [[JA:尾針神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226111}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ohari Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ohari-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|29.25|N|133|54|30.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ohari Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|lt=21st|1=October 21}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ohari Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ohari-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ohari Shrine''' (おはりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity * '''{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}''' - ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}} ; Associated deity * {{ill|lt=Ogetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ogetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}} Regarding the deity, ''{{ill|lt=Kibi Onko Hiroku|Q11412654|WD=Q11412654|1=Kibi Onko Hiroku}}'' also presents theories identifying it as Otsunane-no-Mikoto or {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}}. === History === The shrine stands on a hill to the east-west side of central Okayama. Behind the shrine buildings remains a group of giant rocks regarded as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=iwasaka|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=Haji ware|Q5639938|WD=Q5639938|1=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|lt=Sue ware|Q7634351|WD=Q7634351|1=Sue pottery}} have been excavated from the eastern slope, indicating long-term habitation. Its founding is unknown, but the area was formerly called "Ifuku-go, Mino District, {{ill|lt=Kibi Province|Q1155128|WD=Q1155128|1=Kingdom of Kibi}}," and is said to have been the settlement of the Ifukube no Muraji (a branch of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|lt=Owari no Muraji|1=Owari clan}}). The shrine is said to have begun by enshrining their ancestral deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines).. Nearby stands another shikinaisha connected to the Owari clan, {{ill|lt=Oji Harina Mawakahime Shrine|Q17228003|WD=Q17228003|1=Ojiharina Mawakahime Shrine}}, suggesting a relationship with that clan. There are theories that the Ifukube migrated west from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-era ''Engishiki Naijinsha / Kokushi Genzai no Jinja'' ("Ohari Shrine" section in ''Nihon no Kamigami'')., and conversely theories that the Owari clan migrated east from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Did the ancient Owari clan migrate east from Kibi?" (''Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walk'', Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists "Ohari Shrine, Mino District, {{ill|lt=Bizen Province|Q1053688|WD=Q1053688|1=Bizen Province}}" as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and this shrine is considered one of its {{ill|lt=disputed identifications|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the exact location of that shikinaisha was said to be unknown, and some documents of that era state that the shrine land of Sakaori-no-miya (now {{ill|lt=Okayama Shrine|Q11048799|WD=Q11048799|1=Okayama Shrine}}) was the original precinct. Even now, Ohari Shrine is enshrined among the subordinate shrines of Okayama Shrine, and is regarded as another disputed identification. Until the early Meiji period it was called "Kurioka-no-miya"''Nihon no Kamigami'', section on Ohari Shrine.. In Meiji it was renamed "Ohari Shrine." Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precincts === File:Ohari-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Ohari-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Ohari-jinja shinmon.JPG|Shrine gate File:Ohari-jinja iwasaka.JPG|Iwasaka remains
Located behind the shrine buildings. === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Ohari-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Subsidiary shrines]] * Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} * Wakamiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} * Kikirimiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto|1=Kukunochi}} * Kojin Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Shimenomiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami|1=Ame-no-Uzume}} * Tenmangu - deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Mae-gozen-miya - deity: {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} === Festivals === * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of May) * Summer festival ({{ill|lt=July 29|Q2717|WD=Q2717|1=July 29}}) * Grand annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|lt=21st|1=October 21}}) === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Okayama Station|Q801293|WD=Q801293|1=Okayama Station}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), section on Ohari Shrine in Okayama * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Nihon no Kamigami - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 2 Sanyo/Shikoku'' ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}), section on Ohari Shrine === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ohari-jinja}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1260005000332 == Commons category (P373) == * Ohari-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岡山県岡山市北区京山2-2-2', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Ohari-jinja}} [[JA:尾針神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226111}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ohari Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ohari-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|29.25|N|133|54|30.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ohari Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|lt=21st|1=October 21}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ohari Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ohari-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ohari Shrine''' (おはりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity * '''{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}''' - ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}} ; Associated deity * {{ill|lt=Ogetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ogetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}} Regarding the deity, ''{{ill|lt=Kibi Onko Hiroku|Q11412654|WD=Q11412654|1=Kibi Onko Hiroku}}'' also presents theories identifying it as Otsunane-no-Mikoto or {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}}. === History === The shrine stands on a hill to the east-west side of central Okayama. Behind the shrine buildings remains a group of giant rocks regarded as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=iwasaka|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=Haji ware|Q5639938|WD=Q5639938|1=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|lt=Sue ware|Q7634351|WD=Q7634351|1=Sue pottery}} have been excavated from the eastern slope, indicating long-term habitation. Its founding is unknown, but the area was formerly called "Ifuku-go, Mino District, {{ill|lt=Kibi Province|Q1155128|WD=Q1155128|1=Kingdom of Kibi}}," and is said to have been the settlement of the Ifukube no Muraji (a branch of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|lt=Owari no Muraji|1=Owari clan}}). The shrine is said to have begun by enshrining their ancestral deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines).. Nearby stands another shikinaisha connected to the Owari clan, {{ill|lt=Oji Harina Mawakahime Shrine|Q17228003|WD=Q17228003|1=Ojiharina Mawakahime Shrine}}, suggesting a relationship with that clan. There are theories that the Ifukube migrated west from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-era ''Engishiki Naijinsha / Kokushi Genzai no Jinja'' ("Ohari Shrine" section in ''Nihon no Kamigami'')., and conversely theories that the Owari clan migrated east from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Did the ancient Owari clan migrate east from Kibi?" (''Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walk'', Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists "Ohari Shrine, Mino District, {{ill|lt=Bizen Province|Q1053688|WD=Q1053688|1=Bizen Province}}" as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and this shrine is considered one of its {{ill|lt=disputed identifications|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the exact location of that shikinaisha was said to be unknown, and some documents of that era state that the shrine land of Sakaori-no-miya (now {{ill|lt=Okayama Shrine|Q11048799|WD=Q11048799|1=Okayama Shrine}}) was the original precinct. Even now, Ohari Shrine is enshrined among the subordinate shrines of Okayama Shrine, and is regarded as another disputed identification. Until the early Meiji period it was called "Kurioka-no-miya"''Nihon no Kamigami'', section on Ohari Shrine.. In Meiji it was renamed "Ohari Shrine." Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precincts === File:Ohari-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Ohari-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Ohari-jinja shinmon.JPG|Shrine gate File:Ohari-jinja iwasaka.JPG|Iwasaka remains
Located behind the shrine buildings. === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Ohari-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Subsidiary shrines]] * Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} * Wakamiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} * Kikirimiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto|1=Kukunochi}} * Kojin Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Shimenomiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami|1=Ame-no-Uzume}} * Tenmangu - deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Mae-gozen-miya - deity: {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} === Festivals === * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of May) * Summer festival ({{ill|lt=July 29|Q2717|WD=Q2717|1=July 29}}) * Grand annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|lt=21st|1=October 21}}) === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Okayama Station|Q801293|WD=Q801293|1=Okayama Station}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), section on Ohari Shrine in Okayama * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Nihon no Kamigami - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 2 Sanyo/Shikoku'' ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}), section on Ohari Shrine === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ohari-jinja}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:pages]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1260005000332 == Commons category (P373) == * Ohari-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岡山県岡山市北区京山2-2-2', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Ohari-jinja}} [[JA:尾針神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226111}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ohari Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ohari-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|29.25|N|133|54|30.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ohari Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|lt=21st|1=October 21}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ohari Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ohari-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ohari Shrine''' (おはりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity * '''{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}''' - ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}} ; Associated deity * {{ill|lt=Ogetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ogetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}} Regarding the deity, ''{{ill|lt=Kibi Onko Hiroku|Q11412654|WD=Q11412654|1=Kibi Onko Hiroku}}'' also presents theories identifying it as Otsunane-no-Mikoto or {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}}. === History === The shrine stands on a hill to the east-west side of central Okayama. Behind the shrine buildings remains a group of giant rocks regarded as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=iwasaka|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=Haji ware|Q5639938|WD=Q5639938|1=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|lt=Sue ware|Q7634351|WD=Q7634351|1=Sue pottery}} have been excavated from the eastern slope, indicating long-term habitation. Its founding is unknown, but the area was formerly called "Ifuku-go, Mino District, {{ill|lt=Kibi Province|Q1155128|WD=Q1155128|1=Kingdom of Kibi}}," and is said to have been the settlement of the Ifukube no Muraji (a branch of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|lt=Owari no Muraji|1=Owari clan}}). The shrine is said to have begun by enshrining their ancestral deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines).. Nearby stands another shikinaisha connected to the Owari clan, {{ill|lt=Oji Harina Mawakahime Shrine|Q17228003|WD=Q17228003|1=Ojiharina Mawakahime Shrine}}, suggesting a relationship with that clan. There are theories that the Ifukube migrated west from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-era ''Engishiki Naijinsha / Kokushi Genzai no Jinja'' ("Ohari Shrine" section in ''Nihon no Kamigami'')., and conversely theories that the Owari clan migrated east from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Did the ancient Owari clan migrate east from Kibi?" (''Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walk'', Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists "Ohari Shrine, Mino District, {{ill|lt=Bizen Province|Q1053688|WD=Q1053688|1=Bizen Province}}" as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and this shrine is considered one of its {{ill|lt=disputed identifications|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the exact location of that shikinaisha was said to be unknown, and some documents of that era state that the shrine land of Sakaori-no-miya (now {{ill|lt=Okayama Shrine|Q11048799|WD=Q11048799|1=Okayama Shrine}}) was the original precinct. Even now, Ohari Shrine is enshrined among the subordinate shrines of Okayama Shrine, and is regarded as another disputed identification. Until the early Meiji period it was called "Kurioka-no-miya"''Nihon no Kamigami'', section on Ohari Shrine.. In Meiji it was renamed "Ohari Shrine." Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precincts === File:Ohari-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Ohari-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Ohari-jinja shinmon.JPG|Shrine gate File:Ohari-jinja iwasaka.JPG|Iwasaka remains
Located behind the shrine buildings. === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Ohari-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Subsidiary shrines]] * Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} * Wakamiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} * Kikirimiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto|1=Kukunochi}} * Kojin Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Shimenomiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami|1=Ame-no-Uzume}} * Tenmangu - deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Mae-gozen-miya - deity: {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} === Festivals === * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of May) * Summer festival ({{ill|lt=July 29|Q2717|WD=Q2717|1=July 29}}) * Grand annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|lt=21st|1=October 21}}) === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Okayama Station|Q801293|WD=Q801293|1=Okayama Station}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), section on Ohari Shrine in Okayama * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Nihon no Kamigami - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 2 Sanyo/Shikoku'' ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}), section on Ohari Shrine === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ohari-jinja}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1260005000332 == Commons category (P373) == * Ohari-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岡山県岡山市北区京山2-2-2', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Ohari-jinja}} [[JA:尾針神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226111}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ohari Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ohari-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|29.25|N|133|54|30.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ohari Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|lt=21st|1=October 21}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ohari Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ohari-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ohari Shrine''' (おはりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity * '''{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}''' - ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}} ; Associated deity * {{ill|lt=Ogetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ogetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}} Regarding the deity, ''{{ill|lt=Kibi Onko Hiroku|Q11412654|WD=Q11412654|1=Kibi Onko Hiroku}}'' also presents theories identifying it as Otsunane-no-Mikoto or {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}}. === History === The shrine stands on a hill to the east-west side of central Okayama. Behind the shrine buildings remains a group of giant rocks regarded as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=iwasaka|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=Haji ware|Q5639938|WD=Q5639938|1=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|lt=Sue ware|Q7634351|WD=Q7634351|1=Sue pottery}} have been excavated from the eastern slope, indicating long-term habitation. Its founding is unknown, but the area was formerly called "Ifuku-go, Mino District, {{ill|lt=Kibi Province|Q1155128|WD=Q1155128|1=Kingdom of Kibi}}," and is said to have been the settlement of the Ifukube no Muraji (a branch of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|lt=Owari no Muraji|1=Owari clan}}). The shrine is said to have begun by enshrining their ancestral deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines).. Nearby stands another shikinaisha connected to the Owari clan, {{ill|lt=Oji Harina Mawakahime Shrine|Q17228003|WD=Q17228003|1=Ojiharina Mawakahime Shrine}}, suggesting a relationship with that clan. There are theories that the Ifukube migrated west from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-era ''Engishiki Naijinsha / Kokushi Genzai no Jinja'' ("Ohari Shrine" section in ''Nihon no Kamigami'')., and conversely theories that the Owari clan migrated east from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Did the ancient Owari clan migrate east from Kibi?" (''Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walk'', Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists "Ohari Shrine, Mino District, {{ill|lt=Bizen Province|Q1053688|WD=Q1053688|1=Bizen Province}}" as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and this shrine is considered one of its {{ill|lt=disputed identifications|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the exact location of that shikinaisha was said to be unknown, and some documents of that era state that the shrine land of Sakaori-no-miya (now {{ill|lt=Okayama Shrine|Q11048799|WD=Q11048799|1=Okayama Shrine}}) was the original precinct. Even now, Ohari Shrine is enshrined among the subordinate shrines of Okayama Shrine, and is regarded as another disputed identification. Until the early Meiji period it was called "Kurioka-no-miya"''Nihon no Kamigami'', section on Ohari Shrine.. In Meiji it was renamed "Ohari Shrine." Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precincts === File:Ohari-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Ohari-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Ohari-jinja shinmon.JPG|Shrine gate File:Ohari-jinja iwasaka.JPG|Iwasaka remains
Located behind the shrine buildings. === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Ohari-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Subsidiary shrines]] * Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} * Wakamiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} * Kikirimiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto|1=Kukunochi}} * Kojin Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Shimenomiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami|1=Ame-no-Uzume}} * Tenmangu - deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Mae-gozen-miya - deity: {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} === Festivals === * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of May) * Summer festival ({{ill|lt=July 29|Q2717|WD=Q2717|1=July 29}}) * Grand annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|lt=21st|1=October 21}}) === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Okayama Station|Q801293|WD=Q801293|1=Okayama Station}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), section on Ohari Shrine in Okayama * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Nihon no Kamigami - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 2 Sanyo/Shikoku'' ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}), section on Ohari Shrine === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ohari-jinja}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kita-ku, Okayama]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1260005000332 == Commons category (P373) == * Ohari-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岡山県岡山市北区京山2-2-2', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Ohari-jinja}} [[JA:尾針神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226111}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ohari Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ohari-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|29.25|N|133|54|30.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ohari Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|lt=21st|1=October 21}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ohari Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ohari-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ohari Shrine''' (おはりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity * '''{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}''' - ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}} ; Associated deity * {{ill|lt=Ogetsuhime|Q3261482|WD=Q3261482|lt=Ogetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Ōgetsuhime}} Regarding the deity, ''{{ill|lt=Kibi Onko Hiroku|Q11412654|WD=Q11412654|1=Kibi Onko Hiroku}}'' also presents theories identifying it as Otsunane-no-Mikoto or {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}}. === History === The shrine stands on a hill to the east-west side of central Okayama. Behind the shrine buildings remains a group of giant rocks regarded as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=iwasaka|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=Haji ware|Q5639938|WD=Q5639938|1=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|lt=Sue ware|Q7634351|WD=Q7634351|1=Sue pottery}} have been excavated from the eastern slope, indicating long-term habitation. Its founding is unknown, but the area was formerly called "Ifuku-go, Mino District, {{ill|lt=Kibi Province|Q1155128|WD=Q1155128|1=Kingdom of Kibi}}," and is said to have been the settlement of the Ifukube no Muraji (a branch of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|lt=Owari no Muraji|1=Owari clan}}). The shrine is said to have begun by enshrining their ancestral deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines).. Nearby stands another shikinaisha connected to the Owari clan, {{ill|lt=Oji Harina Mawakahime Shrine|Q17228003|WD=Q17228003|1=Ojiharina Mawakahime Shrine}}, suggesting a relationship with that clan. There are theories that the Ifukube migrated west from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-era ''Engishiki Naijinsha / Kokushi Genzai no Jinja'' ("Ohari Shrine" section in ''Nihon no Kamigami'')., and conversely theories that the Owari clan migrated east from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Did the ancient Owari clan migrate east from Kibi?" (''Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walk'', Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists "Ohari Shrine, Mino District, {{ill|lt=Bizen Province|Q1053688|WD=Q1053688|1=Bizen Province}}" as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and this shrine is considered one of its {{ill|lt=disputed identifications|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the exact location of that shikinaisha was said to be unknown, and some documents of that era state that the shrine land of Sakaori-no-miya (now {{ill|lt=Okayama Shrine|Q11048799|WD=Q11048799|1=Okayama Shrine}}) was the original precinct. Even now, Ohari Shrine is enshrined among the subordinate shrines of Okayama Shrine, and is regarded as another disputed identification. Until the early Meiji period it was called "Kurioka-no-miya"''Nihon no Kamigami'', section on Ohari Shrine.. In Meiji it was renamed "Ohari Shrine." Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precincts === File:Ohari-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Ohari-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Ohari-jinja shinmon.JPG|Shrine gate File:Ohari-jinja iwasaka.JPG|Iwasaka remains
Located behind the shrine buildings. === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Ohari-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Subsidiary shrines]] * Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} * Wakamiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} * Kikirimiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto|1=Kukunochi}} * Kojin Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Shimenomiya - deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami|1=Ame-no-Uzume}} * Tenmangu - deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Mae-gozen-miya - deity: {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} === Festivals === * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of May) * Summer festival ({{ill|lt=July 29|Q2717|WD=Q2717|1=July 29}}) * Grand annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|lt=21st|1=October 21}}) === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Kyoyama 2-2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Okayama Station|Q801293|WD=Q801293|1=Okayama Station}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), section on Ohari Shrine in Okayama * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Nihon no Kamigami - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 2 Sanyo/Shikoku'' ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}), section on Ohari Shrine === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ohari-jinja}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01091 Ohari Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ohari Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kita-ku, Okayama]]
